requirementCueLines: Admission Requirements
Admission is capacity constrained. Completion of minimum requirements described below does not guarantee admission. The application and additional information are available at the department website.

Construction Management has three admission pathways: Freshmen Direct, Early Admission, and Upper-Division Admission. Transfer students apply for admission under the Upper-Division Admission pathway. The application and additional information are available at the department website.
Complete one of the two following prior to applying:
ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219
MATH 112 or MATH 124
5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
ECON 200
PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141)
5 additional credits from any NSc course
Prerequisite Courses: Must be completed by the beginning of autumn quarter for admission that quarter:
Arts and Humanities (A&H) (10 credits): the following is recommended: CM 250; additional A&H credits from ARCH, BE, CEP, L ARCH, RE, URBDP prefixed courses
Social Sciences (SSc) (10 credits): ECON 200 and MGMT 200

Natural Sciences (NSc) (24-25 credits): PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141), PHYS 115 and 118 (or PHYS 122 or PHYS 142); one of MATH 112, MATH 124, or Q SCI 291; ESS 101; one of QMETH 201, STAT 220, or STAT 311
English Composition (5 credits): 5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
Reasoning (RSN) (5 credits)
Writing (W) (5 credits)
Diversity (DIV) (5 credits)
Construction: CM 260
Communications: CM 220 or COM 220
Business and Management: ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219; MGMT 200 (counts toward SSc requirement)
Transfer students apply for admission under this pathway.
Completion Requirements
Minimum 180 credits
General Education Requirements
Arts and Humanities (A&H) (10 credits): the following is recommended: CM 250; additional A&H credits from ARCH, B E, CEP, L ARCH, R E, URBDP prefixed courses
Writing (W) (8 credits): CM 301; 5 credits additional composition or W courses
Major Requirements
Minimum 95 credits
Lower-Division Coursework (17-18 credits): Required for admission, but not counted toward the minimum 95 major credits
Construction (3 credits): CM 260
Communications (5 credits): CM 220 or COM 220
Business and Management (9-10 credits): ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219; MGMT 200 (counts toward SSc requirement)

Foundation Courses (78 credits): ARCH 320, ARCH 321, CM 301 (counts toward W requirement), CM 310, CM 312, CM 313, CM 314, CM 320, CM 321, CM 322, CM 323, CM 331, CM 333, CM 335, CM 343, CM 410, CM 412, CM 414, CM 418, CM 420, CM 421, CM 423, CM 424, CM 429, CM 434
Business and Management (4-5 credits): MGMT 300 or MGMT 305
Capstone Experience (7 credits): CM 427, CM 431(minimum 2.0 grade)
Construction Electives (minimum 6 credits ): see department website for list of approved courses

All students must make satisfactory academic progress in the major. Failure to do so results in major-level academic notification, major-level warning, and major-level alerts, which can lead to dismissal from the major and being precluded from subsequently declaring the major in a future quarter. For the complete continuation policy, contact the departmental adviser or refer to the department website.

requirementCueLines: Admission Requirements
Admission is capacity constrained. Completion of minimum requirements described below does not guarantee admission. The application and additional information are available at the department website.

Construction Management has three admission pathways: Freshmen Direct, Early Admission, and Upper-Division Admission. Transfer students apply for admission under the Upper-Division Admission pathway. The application and additional information are available at the department website.
Complete one of the two following prior to applying:
ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219
MATH 112 or MATH 124
5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
ECON 200
PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141)
5 additional credits from any NSc course
Prerequisite Courses: Must be completed by the beginning of autumn quarter for admission that quarter:
Arts and Humanities (A&H) (10 credits): the following is recommended: CM 250; additional A&H credits from ARCH, BE, CEP, L ARCH, RE, URBDP prefixed courses
Social Sciences (SSc) (10 credits): ECON 200 and MGMT 200

Natural Sciences (NSc) (24-25 credits): PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141), PHYS 115 and 118 (or PHYS 122 or PHYS 142); one of MATH 112, MATH 124, or Q SCI 291; ESS 101; one of QMETH 201, STAT 220, or STAT 311
English Composition (5 credits): 5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
Reasoning (RSN) (5 credits)
Writing (W) (5 credits)
Diversity (DIV) (5 credits)
Construction: CM 260
Communications: CM 220 or COM 220
Business and Management: ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219; MGMT 200 (counts toward SSc requirement)
Transfer students apply for admission under this pathway.
Completion Requirements
Minimum 180 credits
General Education Requirements
Arts and Humanities (A&H) (10 credits): the following is recommended: CM 250; additional A&H credits from ARCH, B E, CEP, L ARCH, R E, URBDP prefixed courses
Writing (W) (8 credits): CM 301; 5 credits additional composition or W courses
Major Requirements
Minimum 95 credits
Lower-Division Coursework (17-18 credits): Required for admission, but not counted toward the minimum 95 major credits
Construction (3 credits): CM 260
Communications (5 credits): CM 220 or COM 220
Business and Management (9-10 credits): ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219; MGMT 200 (counts toward SSc requirement)

Foundation Courses (78 credits): ARCH 320, ARCH 321, CM 301 (counts toward W requirement), CM 310, CM 312, CM 313, CM 314, CM 320, CM 321, CM 322, CM 323, CM 331, CM 333, CM 335, CM 343, CM 410, CM 412, CM 414, CM 418, CM 420, CM 421, CM 423, CM 424, CM 429, CM 434
Business and Management (4-5 credits): MGMT 300 or MGMT 305
Capstone Experience (7 credits): CM 427, CM 431(minimum 2.0 grade)
Construction Electives (minimum 6 credits ): see department website for list of approved courses

All students must make satisfactory academic progress in the major. Failure to do so results in major-level academic notification, major-level warning, and major-level alerts, which can lead to dismissal from the major and being precluded from subsequently declaring the major in a future quarter. For the complete continuation policy, contact the departmental adviser or refer to the department website.

parsedDegreeMapBlockCandidates.title: Construction Management - Early Admission pathway parsed official source requirements
sourceLineHints: Admission Requirements
Admission is capacity constrained. Completion of minimum requirements described below does not guarantee admission. The application and additional information are available at the department website.

Construction Management has three admission pathways: Freshmen Direct, Early Admission, and Upper-Division Admission. Transfer students apply for admission under the Upper-Division Admission pathway. The application and additional information are available at the department website.
Complete one of the two following prior to applying:
ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219
MATH 112 or MATH 124
5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
ECON 200
PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141)
5 additional credits from any NSc course

requirementCueLines: Admission Requirements
Admission is capacity constrained. Completion of minimum requirements described below does not guarantee admission. The application and additional information are available at the department website.

Construction Management has three admission pathways: Freshmen Direct, Early Admission, and Upper-Division Admission. Transfer students apply for admission under the Upper-Division Admission pathway. The application and additional information are available at the department website.
Complete one of the two following prior to applying:
ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219
MATH 112 or MATH 124
5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
ECON 200
PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141)
5 additional credits from any NSc course
Prerequisite Courses: Must be completed by the beginning of autumn quarter for admission that quarter:
Arts and Humanities (A&H) (10 credits): the following is recommended: CM 250; additional A&H credits from ARCH, BE, CEP, L ARCH, RE, URBDP prefixed courses
Social Sciences (SSc) (10 credits): ECON 200 and MGMT 200

Natural Sciences (NSc) (24-25 credits): PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141), PHYS 115 and 118 (or PHYS 122 or PHYS 142); one of MATH 112, MATH 124, or Q SCI 291; ESS 101; one of QMETH 201, STAT 220, or STAT 311
English Composition (5 credits): 5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
Reasoning (RSN) (5 credits)
Writing (W) (5 credits)
Diversity (DIV) (5 credits)
Construction: CM 260
Communications: CM 220 or COM 220
Business and Management: ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219; MGMT 200 (counts toward SSc requirement)
Transfer students apply for admission under this pathway.
Completion Requirements
Minimum 180 credits
General Education Requirements
Arts and Humanities (A&H) (10 credits): the following is recommended: CM 250; additional A&H credits from ARCH, B E, CEP, L ARCH, R E, URBDP prefixed courses
Writing (W) (8 credits): CM 301; 5 credits additional composition or W courses
Major Requirements
Minimum 95 credits
Lower-Division Coursework (17-18 credits): Required for admission, but not counted toward the minimum 95 major credits
Construction (3 credits): CM 260
Communications (5 credits): CM 220 or COM 220
Business and Management (9-10 credits): ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219; MGMT 200 (counts toward SSc requirement)

Foundation Courses (78 credits): ARCH 320, ARCH 321, CM 301 (counts toward W requirement), CM 310, CM 312, CM 313, CM 314, CM 320, CM 321, CM 322, CM 323, CM 331, CM 333, CM 335, CM 343, CM 410, CM 412, CM 414, CM 418, CM 420, CM 421, CM 423, CM 424, CM 429, CM 434
Business and Management (4-5 credits): MGMT 300 or MGMT 305
Capstone Experience (7 credits): CM 427, CM 431(minimum 2.0 grade)
Construction Electives (minimum 6 credits ): see department website for list of approved courses

All students must make satisfactory academic progress in the major. Failure to do so results in major-level academic notification, major-level warning, and major-level alerts, which can lead to dismissal from the major and being precluded from subsequently declaring the major in a future quarter. For the complete continuation policy, contact the departmental adviser or refer to the department website.

parsedDegreeMapBlockCandidates.title: Construction Management - Freshmen Direct pathway parsed official source requirements
sourceLineHints: Admission Requirements
Admission is capacity constrained. Completion of minimum requirements described below does not guarantee admission. The application and additional information are available at the department website.

Construction Management has three admission pathways: Freshmen Direct, Early Admission, and Upper-Division Admission. Transfer students apply for admission under the Upper-Division Admission pathway. The application and additional information are available at the department website.
Complete one of the two following prior to applying:
ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219
MATH 112 or MATH 124
5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
ECON 200
PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141)
5 additional credits from any NSc course

requirementCueLines: Admission Requirements
Admission is capacity constrained. Completion of minimum requirements described below does not guarantee admission. The application and additional information are available at the department website.

Construction Management has three admission pathways: Freshmen Direct, Early Admission, and Upper-Division Admission. Transfer students apply for admission under the Upper-Division Admission pathway. The application and additional information are available at the department website.
Complete one of the two following prior to applying:
ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219
MATH 112 or MATH 124
5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
ECON 200
PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141)
5 additional credits from any NSc course
Prerequisite Courses: Must be completed by the beginning of autumn quarter for admission that quarter:
Arts and Humanities (A&H) (10 credits): the following is recommended: CM 250; additional A&H credits from ARCH, BE, CEP, L ARCH, RE, URBDP prefixed courses
Social Sciences (SSc) (10 credits): ECON 200 and MGMT 200

Natural Sciences (NSc) (24-25 credits): PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141), PHYS 115 and 118 (or PHYS 122 or PHYS 142); one of MATH 112, MATH 124, or Q SCI 291; ESS 101; one of QMETH 201, STAT 220, or STAT 311
English Composition (5 credits): 5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
Reasoning (RSN) (5 credits)
Writing (W) (5 credits)
Diversity (DIV) (5 credits)
Construction: CM 260
Communications: CM 220 or COM 220
Business and Management: ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219; MGMT 200 (counts toward SSc requirement)
Transfer students apply for admission under this pathway.
Completion Requirements
Minimum 180 credits
General Education Requirements
Arts and Humanities (A&H) (10 credits): the following is recommended: CM 250; additional A&H credits from ARCH, B E, CEP, L ARCH, R E, URBDP prefixed courses
Writing (W) (8 credits): CM 301; 5 credits additional composition or W courses
Major Requirements
Minimum 95 credits
Lower-Division Coursework (17-18 credits): Required for admission, but not counted toward the minimum 95 major credits
Construction (3 credits): CM 260
Communications (5 credits): CM 220 or COM 220
Business and Management (9-10 credits): ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219; MGMT 200 (counts toward SSc requirement)

Foundation Courses (78 credits): ARCH 320, ARCH 321, CM 301 (counts toward W requirement), CM 310, CM 312, CM 313, CM 314, CM 320, CM 321, CM 322, CM 323, CM 331, CM 333, CM 335, CM 343, CM 410, CM 412, CM 414, CM 418, CM 420, CM 421, CM 423, CM 424, CM 429, CM 434
Business and Management (4-5 credits): MGMT 300 or MGMT 305
Capstone Experience (7 credits): CM 427, CM 431(minimum 2.0 grade)
Construction Electives (minimum 6 credits ): see department website for list of approved courses

All students must make satisfactory academic progress in the major. Failure to do so results in major-level academic notification, major-level warning, and major-level alerts, which can lead to dismissal from the major and being precluded from subsequently declaring the major in a future quarter. For the complete continuation policy, contact the departmental adviser or refer to the department website.

parsedDegreeMapBlockCandidates.title: Construction Management - Upper-Division Admission pathway parsed official source requirements
sourceLineHints: Admission Requirements
Admission is capacity constrained. Completion of minimum requirements described below does not guarantee admission. The application and additional information are available at the department website.

Construction Management has three admission pathways: Freshmen Direct, Early Admission, and Upper-Division Admission. Transfer students apply for admission under the Upper-Division Admission pathway. The application and additional information are available at the department website.
Complete one of the two following prior to applying:
ACCTG 215 or ACCTG 219
MATH 112 or MATH 124
5 credits from English composition list (ENGL 131 recommended)
ECON 200
PHYS 114 and PHYS 117 (or PHYS 121 or PHYS 141)
5 additional credits from any NSc course
